questions.question: A train travels at a certain average speed for a distance of $360$ km. If the speed had been $5$ km/hr more, it would have taken $1$ hour less for the same journey. Find the original average speed of the train. If the speed is further increased by $x$ km/hr such that the total time taken for the journey is $2$ hours less than the original time, find the value of $x$.

steps: Let the original speed of the train be $v$ km/hr.
The time taken to cover $360$ km is $t_1 = 360/v$.
With increased speed $(v+5)$, the time taken is $t_2 = 360/(v+5)$. Given $t_1 - t_2 = 1$, we form the equation $360/v - 360/(v+5) = 1$.
Solving this quadratic $v^2 + 5v - 1800 = 0$ gives $v = 40$ or $v = -45$. Rejecting the negative value, the original speed is $40$ km/hr.
For the second condition, let the new speed be $v_3 = 40 + x$. The time taken is $360/(40+x) = 360/40 - 2 = 9 - 2 = 7$.
Solving $360/(40+x) = 7$ yields $40+x = 360/7$, which simplifies to $x = 360/7 - 280/7 = 80/7$ km/hr.
solution.derivation: $t_1 - t_2 = 1 \Rightarrow \frac{360}{v} - \frac{360}{v+5} = 1 \Rightarrow 360(v+5) - 360v = v(v+5) \Rightarrow 1800 = v^2 + 5v \Rightarrow v^2 + 5v - 1800 = 0 \Rightarrow (v + 45)(v - 40) = 0 \Rightarrow v = 40 \text{ (since } v > 0) \\ \text{New condition: } \frac{360}{40+x} = \frac{360}{40} - 2 \Rightarrow \frac{360}{40+x} = 9 - 2 = 7 \Rightarrow 360 = 7(40+x) \Rightarrow 360 = 280 + 7x \Rightarrow 7x = 80 \Rightarrow x = \frac{80}{7} \approx 11.43$

retrieved_sources: Chapter_3 (Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables).pdf
